apricot-lavender-jam.name: Apricot Lavender Jam

ingredients.prep: such as Sure-Jell® Pectin Light for less or no sugar

instructions: Combine ½ cup sugar and lavender blossoms in the bowl of a food processor; pulse until lavender blossoms are finely chopped and sugar is fragrant.

Measure 5 cups sliced apricots; finely chop and place into a plastic or glass bowl with a tight-fitting lid. Stir in lavender sugar and lemon juice; mix well. Cover the bowl; refrigerate for 8 hours to overnight.
Inspect 8 half-pint jars for cracks and rings for rust, discarding any defective ones. Immerse in simmering water until jam is ready. Wash new, unused lids and rings in warm soapy water.

Stir ¼ cup sugar and pectin together in a bowl; stir into apricots. Transfer apricot mixture to a large saucepan; mix well. Slowly bring to a full rolling boil that does not stop bubbling when stirred. Stir in remaining 3 ¾ cups sugar, scraping the bottom of the pan, to fully dissolve sugar. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.

Pack hot jam into the prepared jars, filling to within ¼ inch of tops. Run a clean knife or thin spatula around insides of jars to remove any air bubbles. Wipe rims with a moist paper towel to remove any residue. Top with lids and tightly screw on rings.

Place a rack in the bottom of a large stockpot and fill halfway with water; bring to a boil. Lower jars 2 inches apart into boiling water using a holder. Pour in more boiling water to cover jars by at least 1 inch. Bring to a rolling boil, cover, and process for 10 minutes.

Remove jars from the stockpot and place on a cloth-covered or wood surface, several inches apart. Rest for 24 hours without moving jars. Gently press center of each lid with a finger to ensure the lid does not move up or down. Remove rings for storage. Store in a cool, dark area.

apricot-linzertorte-with-quark-whipped-c.name: Apricot Linzertorte With Quark Whipped Cream
instructions: Preheat oven to 191°C. Butter 10-inch springform pan. Process nuts and 1/4 cup flour in processor until nuts are finely ground. Transfer to medium bowl. Whisk in 1 cup flour, cocoa powder, cinnamon, 3/4 teaspoon salt, and cloves. Using electric mixer, beat 14 tablespoons butter and 1 1/4 cups sugar in large bowl. Beat in egg, then egg yolk and orange peel. Gradually stir in dry ingredients. Spoon 1 1/2 cups batter into pastry bag fitted with 3/8-inch plain round tip; set aside. Spread remaining batter in pan.
Bake torte bottom until golden, about 35 minutes. Cool in pan on rack 10 minutes. Maintain oven temperature.

Using some of batter in pastry bag, pipe border around edge of torte bottom. Spread preserves evenly over torte bottom. Pipe remaining batter over preserves in lattice pattern, 5 to 6 strips in each direction. Bake until lattice is firm to touch and preserves are bubbling, about 40 minutes. Cool completely. DO AHEAD Can be made 3 days ahead. Store airtight at room temperature.
Beat cream in bowl until peaks form; beat in quark. Dust torte with powdered sugar. Serve with quark whipped cream.
If you don't have a pastry bag, spoon the batter into a resealable plastic bag. Twist bag at top, grasp top firmly, and cut off enough of one corner to form 3/8-inch-wide opening.

apricot-miso-jam.name: Apricot-miso Jam
instructions: Combine apricots, cherries, sake or wine, orange juice, lemon juice, and 1 cup water in a large saucepan; let soak for 2 hours.

Place star anise, bay leaf, cinnamon, and ginger in the center of a layer of cheesecloth. Gather up edges; tie with kitchen twine to form a bundle. Add to pan. Bring to a simmer over medium heat; reduce heat to low and cook, stirring often, for 30 minutes.
Stir in miso. Continue to cook, stirring occasionally, until mixture is thickened, 5–10 minutes. Let cool.

apricot-peach-liqueur-argiro.name: Apricot Peach Liqueur
instructions: Choose ripe but firm fruit without bumps and wash them.
Cut the apricots in half, remove their pits and place them in a pot.
Clean the peaches, cut them into pieces, remove the pits and add them to the pot.
Sprinkle the fruit with the sugar and add the orange peel.
Leave the mixture for 10 minutes until the sugar melts (they must not boil!).
Once our fruits cool, we put them in a sterile jar.
Add the cognac and liqueur and mix.

apricot-pie.name: Apricot Pie
instructions: Preheat the oven to 218°C (220 degrees C).

To make the dough: Mix flour and salt together in a bowl. Cut in shortening with a pastry cutter until mixture is crumbly. Using a fork, mix in water, 1 tablespoon at a time, until dough just holds together. Divide dough in half and form each half into a ball.

Roll each ball out on a floured surface into a crust big enough for a 9-inch pie dish with an inch or so to spare. Carefully lift one crust, fold it into quarters, position it into a 9-inch pie dish, and unfold to fit. Set second crust aside.

To make the filling: Mix 1 cup sugar, flour, and cinnamon together in a large bowl until thoroughly combined. Stir in apricots and lemon juice. Spoon filling over bottom crust, then top with remaining crust. Crimp crust edges together with a fork and cut away any excess. Cut slits into top crust to allow steam to escape. Cover the edges with strips of aluminum foil to prevent burning.

Bake on the center rack of the preheated oven until the pie is browned and the filling is bubbling and thickened, 35 to 45 minutes. Remove to a rack to cool and sprinkle top with remaining 1 teaspoon of sugar.

apricot-pudding-lezzet.name: Apricot Pudding

ingredients.item: 1 cup of tea muzzle antep nuts

instructions: 1. Crush starch and flour in 2 spoons of water.
2. Eggs, sugar, starchy mixture and freshly squeezed apricot juice into the pot and cook the materials with mixer to seven.
3. Cook until it comes to the consistency of the opponent by constantly stirring in the ground fire.
4. Cook for a few minutes and cook from the stove.
5. Cool the fire with the mixer.
6. Wet the shaped molds light and share the opponent.
7. Wait for (1 night) until thoroughly solidified in the refrigerator.
8. Eliminate apricots and remove their cores.
9. Take the pan and add sugar.
10. Bake Kı on the ground fire.
11. 11. Leave to cool.
12. Decorate and serve the uniforms of the people.

apricot-rice-pudding-pops.name: Apricot Rice Pudding Pops

ingredients.item: Plus 2 tbsp. canned unsweetened coconut milk

ingredients.prep: tough outer layer removed, thinly sliced

instructions: Combine milk, coconut milk, cream, lemongrass, ginger, and 1/2 cup water in a medium saucepan. Scrape in seeds from vanilla bean; add bean. Bring to a boil; remove from heat, cover, and let steep for 15 minutes.
Meanwhile, place rice and 1/2 cup water in a small bowl; let stand for 10 minutes (to soften and release some starch). Drain.

Strain coconut-milk mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a medium saucepan. Add rice; bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer (do not stir or rice will become too starchy) until rice is very tender, 30-35 minutes. Let cool slightly.

Stir in apricots and sugar. Divide mixture among ice-pop molds. Freeze until beginning to set, 30-45 minutes. Insert a stick into each pop. Freeze until firm, about 1 hour longer. DO AHEAD: Pops can be made 2 weeks ahead. Keep frozen.

apricot-sorbet-floats.name: Apricot Sorbet Floats

ingredients.item: Sparkling wine plus more for serving

instructions: Bring sugar, 1 pound apricots, ¾ cup sparkling wine, and 2 cups water to a boil in a medium saucepan. Reduce heat and simmer, stirring occasionally, until apricots are very tender, 10-15 minutes. Let cool completely.

Transfer apricot mixture to a blender and purée until smooth. Add water to apricot mixture so it measures 4 cups. Transfer mixture to a large shallow baking dish. Freeze until solid, at least 4 hours.
To serve, scoop sorbet into coupe glasses or bowls and top with more apricot slices and some sparkling wine.

apricot-sponge-cake-teleculinaria.name: Apricot Sponge Cake
instructions: Garnish with butter a round shape 22 cm in diameter and sprinkle it with flour. In a bowl, beat the butter softened with sugar. Add the flour mixed with yeast, then the eggs and stir well.
On the other hand, beat the clears into castle and wrap in the previous mass. Lay in the form, reserving about 1/4, and take it to the oven, preheated to 180°C, for 20 minutes.
Wash the apricots thoroughly, remove their pit and cut the pulp into cubes. Mix with the almond biscuits, roughly crushed, the dough you reserved and the liquid caramel.

After the specified oven time, lay the previous dough on the cake and sprinkle with the rolled almonds. Take it back to the oven for another 25 minutes. Check the cooking with a toothpick, remove, let cool, peel and serve.

apricot-tart-with-whiskey-pecan-frangipa.name: Apricot Tart With Whiskey-pecan Frangipane

ingredients.item: Diamond crystal or  0.25 tsp. morton kosher salt
Diamond crystal or  0.5 tsp. morton kosher salt
A 9"-diameter tart pan with removable bottom

instructions: Pulse powdered sugar, salt, and 1½ cups (188 g) all-purpose flour in a food processor just to combine. Add butter and pulse until largest pieces are pea-size. Whisk egg yolks and 1 Tbsp. water in a small bowl to blend. Drizzle into food processor and pulse until a crumbly mixture the texture of wet sand forms. Using your hands or the lightly floured bottom of a measuring cup, press mixture into bottom and all the way up sides of pan (it might just barely hold together). Freeze crust until firm, at least 20 minutes.

Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to 350°. Prick bottom of crust in several places with a fork and bake until set and lightly browned around the edges, 20–25 minutes. Transfer to a wire rack and let cool 10 minutes.
Do ahead: Crust can be made (but not baked) 12 hours ahead. Wrap tightly and keep frozen. Bake from frozen.
Toast pecans on a rimmed baking sheet, tossing halfway through, until slightly darkened and fragrant, 8–10 minutes. Let cool.

Transfer pecans to a food processor and add egg, granulated sugar, salt, butter, whiskey, and vanilla and process until creamy and mostly smooth (some small pecan pieces are okay; frangipane should be spreadable).

Spread frangipane across bottom of crust. Arrange apricots, cut side down, on top, gently pressing into frangipane. Bake tart until frangipane is puffed and just set and crust is golden brown, 25–30 minutes. Let cool, then remove tart from pan and slice into 8 wedges to serve.
Do ahead: Frangipane can be made 1 day ahead. Transfer to an airtight container; cover and chill. Bring to room temperature before using.

apricot-cherry-and-graham-cracker-crumbl.name: Apricot, Cherry, And Graham Cracker Crumble
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°. Combine apricots, cherries, cornstarch, 2 Tbsp. brown sugar, and a pinch of salt in a large bowl. Scrape in seeds from vanilla bean; reserve pod for another use. Toss to combine and transfer to a shallow 1 ½-qt. baking dish.

Combine crackers, butter, and a pinch of salt in a medium bowl. Work butter into crackers with your fingers until mixture comes together. Add nuts and remaining 2 Tbsp. brown sugar and work in with your fingers until combined. Scatter over fruit.
Bake crumble until topping is golden brown and fruit is bubbling, 35–45 minutes. Let cool slightly before serving.

apricot-peach-smoothie-argiro.name: Apricot, Peach Smoothie

ingredients.item: 1cup of yogurt (200 g) with peach flavor or any fruit you like
1cup of yogurt (200 g) with peach flavor or any fruit you like

instructions: Add the ice cubes, milk and yogurt to the blender first and beat them for a few seconds to fluffy.
Wash the apricots, nectarines and peach well.
Cut them in half and remove the pits.
Clean and chop all the fruits.
Add them to the blender and continue hitting for 30′′, until it becomes a uniform, creamy mixture.
Divide the smoothie apricot into 2 glasses and garnish with slices of fruit or mint leaves
Discover more fast recipes and ideas for nutrients smoothies.

apricot-anise-tarts.name: Apricot-anise Tarts
instructions: Combine apricots and 2 cups water in a large heavy saucepan. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to medium, and simmer, stir-ring and mashing apricots occasionally, until fruit is very soft and broken down and most of liquid is evaporated, about 15 minutes. Add 1/2 cup sugar; stir until dissolved. Remove from heat and stir in preserves and 1 tsp. almond extract. Let cool completely. DO AHEAD Can be made 3 days ahead. Cover and chill.
Follow directions for Master Sweet Dough, adding aniseed as directed.

Punch down dough; divide in half. Place half of dough in the center of a 12-inch-square piece of parchment paper. Using your fingertips, shape dough into a 9-inch round. Slide dough on parchment paper onto one end of a large baking sheet. Repeat with remaining dough on another piece of parchment; transfer to other end of baking sheet. Divide apricot mixture between rounds, leaving a 3/4 inch plain border.
Loosely cover tarts with plastic wrap or a kitchen towel and let rise in a warm, draft-free area until puffed but not doubled in size, 45–50 minutes.
Meanwhile, arrange a rack in middle of oven; preheat to 375°. Beat egg with 2 tsp. warm water in a small bowl.

Brush border of dough with egg wash. Bake tarts until crusts are golden, 25–30 minutes. Transfer to a wire rack. DO AHEAD: Tarts can be baked 1 day ahead. Let cool com-pletely. Wrap in foil and store at room temperature. Reheat in a 350° oven until warm, 10–15 minutes. Alternatively, freeze in a resealable freezer bag for up to 1 month. Thaw before reheating.

Dust hot tarts with powdered sugar. Let cool slightly. Using an electric mixer, beat cream, remaining 1/4 cup sugar, and remaining 1/4 tsp. almond extract in a medium bowl until soft peaks form. Serve tarts warm or at room temperature with almond whipped cream.

apricot-date-bars.name: Apricot-date Bars
instructions: Preheat oven to 177°C (175 degrees C).
Mix oats, flour, 1 cup brown sugar, baking soda, and salt together in a large bowl. Stir in butter until mixture is crumbly.
Press a little over half of the crumbly mixture into the bottom of a 10x15-inch baking pan.
Bake in the preheated oven until starting to brown, 10 to 15 minutes.

Combine remaining 1/2 cup brown sugar, dates, apricots, and orange juice in a saucepan over medium heat. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat; cook and stir until thickened, breaking up any clumps, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat and stir in vanilla extract. Cool filling slightly, about 5 minutes.
Drop spoonfuls of warm filling over the crust. Spread evenly with an offset spatula. Sprinkle remaining crumbly mixture evenly over the filling; press gently into the filling.
Bake in the preheated oven until top is lightly browned, about 25 minutes. Cut into 1-1/4x2-inch bars with a pastry cutter while still hot. Cool bars in the pan, about 20 minutes.

apricot-glazed-pork-ribs.name: Apricot-glazed Pork Ribs

ingredients.item: Salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Place ribs in a large roasting pan. Whisk apricot preserves, soy sauce, brown sugar, and garlic powder together until combined; pour over ribs, cover, and marinate in the refrigerator, 8 hours to overnight.
Preheat the oven to 163°C (165 degrees C). Line a baking pan with aluminum foil.

Remove ribs from marinade; shake off excess. Place ribs in the prepared baking pan; season with salt and black pepper. Pour marinade into a small saucepan; bring to a boil. Reduce heat to medium-low; simmer for 5 minutes. Set aside.

Bake ribs in the preheated oven until tender and meat easily pulls away from the bone, about 1 ½ hours, basting frequently with marinade. An instant-read thermometer inserted into the center should read 63°C (63 degrees C).

apricot-pineapple-dream.name: Apricot-pineapple Dream
instructions: Mix orange-flavored gelatin with pineapple in a small saucepan. Cook over low heat until gelatin is completely dissolved. Remove from heat and stir in sugar. Cool completely.

In a medium mixing bowl, using a mixer on medium speed, beat gelatin mixture, baby food, cream cheese and whipped topping. Blend well. Stir in nuts. Spoon into serving bowl, cover, and refrigerate at least four hours.

apricot-pistachio-muffins-baked-on-the-g.name: Apricot-pistachio Muffins Baked On The Grill
instructions: Set up a grill for indirect grilling, with only half or two-thirds of the burners lit on a gas grill or with the coals on one side. Bring the grill temperature to about 450°. Line a 12-cup muffin tin with paper or foil liners.
In a large bowl, whisk the flour with the cornmeal, salt, baking powder and baking soda.
In a medium bowl, whisk the sugar with the eggs, sour cream and butter; mix into the dry ingredients, then fold in the apricots and pistachios. The batter will be quite thick.

Spoon the batter into the muffin cups, filling each cup almost to the top. Set the muffin tin on the unlit side of the grill, close the lid and bake the muffins for 20 to 25 minutes, keeping the grill temperature between 400° and 450° and turning the pan once halfway through. The muffins are done when they are lightly browned on top and when a toothpick inserted in them comes out with a few moist crumbs attached.
Carefully transfer the muffins to a rack and let cool slightly. Serve warm or at room temperature with yogurt.

apricot-riesling-jam.name: Apricot-riesling Jam

ingredients.item: Two 1-pint canning jars or four 8-oz. canning jars with lids

instructions: Combine apricots, sugar, and Riesling in a large heavy pot, stirring to dissolve sugar. Let sit until apricots start to release their juices, 20–30 minutes.

Cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally at first and then more often as mixture thickens (as sugars concentrate, jam will be more likely to scorch), until most of the liquid evaporates and mixture is thickened, 30–40 minutes. To test for thickness, place a small dollop of jam on a chilled plate and chill 2 minutes. Drag your finger through: It should leave a clear path that doesn’t fill in. Divide between jars, cover, and chill.
Do Ahead: Jam can be made 2 months ahead. Keep chilled.

aprils-spicy-chickpea-soup-with-kale.name: April's Spicy Chickpea Soup With Kale

ingredients.item: Salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Heat olive oil over medium heat in a medium-sized pot until warm. Add cumin seeds and red pepper flakes; cook and stir until fragrant, about 30 seconds. Add onion, carrot, celery, and garlic; cook and stir until onion is softened and translucent, about 5 minutes. Stir in chicken broth, chickpeas, and kale.
Bring soup to a simmer and cook until kale is tender, 10 to 15 minutes. Season with salt and pepper; stir in parsley. Remove from heat; top with Parmesan cheese.

aquafaba-mayonnaise.name: Aquafaba Mayonnaise
instructions: Place aquafaba, apple cider vinegar, salt, mustard, and stevia in a tall Mason jar. Using an immersion blender, start blending while slowly streaming in the oil. Blend until mayonnaise is thick and creamy, about 2 minutes.

aquavit-spritzer.name: Aquavit Spritzer
instructions: Combine sugar and 1 Tbsp. hot water in a pitcher. Stir to dissolve. Add preserves. Using a wooden spoon, stir to loosen preserves and mix with simple syrup. Gently stir in soda water and aquavit.
Fill tumblers with ice. Divide aquavit spritzer equally among glasses and garnish each with a lemon twist.

aquavit-toddy.name: Aquavit Toddy

ingredients.item: Unfiltered apple juice or apple cider

instructions: Cook star anise, cardamom, and black peppercorns in a dry small saucepan over medium heat, stirring and crushing spices against side of saucepan, until fragrant, about 2 minutes. Add sugar and 2 Tbsp. water and bring to a simmer, stirring to dissolve sugar. Let cool. Strain into an airtight container and stir in honey (you should have about ¼ cup).
DO AHEAD: Syrup can be made 1 week ahead. Cover and chill.

Heat apple juice and spiced honey syrup in a small saucepan over medium-low, stirring occasionally, until steaming. Remove from heat and stir in aquavit and lemon juice. Serve in mugs garnished with star anise.

arabas-soup-lezzet.name: Caring Sauce Tags
instructions: 1. For dough, boil 1 teaspoon of salt in a large saucepan 5 cups of water added.
2. Mix the remaining 2 cups of cold water and make the slurry.
3. Quickly add it to the boiling water.

10. Cook until the meat is softened and cut it into the size of the finger.

arabic-meatballs-recipe-lezzet.name: Arabic Meatballs Recipe

You can make köfte is softer and juicy by prefering oily meat.

You can get different flavors by adding bitter pepper, cumin or thyme instead.
Instead of onion you can enrich the flavor by adding garlic or fresh onions.
K&ouml can cook in the oven instead of roasting thefts; this yöntem gives less oily endç
It provides a lighter version, soak in less fat in the pan instead of frying.
K&ouml can offer thefts in one service plates or you can offer in bulk in a büyük service plate.
You can create different presentations by serving with yogurt sauce, tomato sauce or közed vegetables.

instructions: 1. Take a large container of Bulguru and wait for about 10 minutes by boiling water d&ouml on the car.

arabic-pie-argiro.name: Arabic Pie

ingredients.item: 2 cup flour for all uses (or mixed with whole flour)
Water as long as it takes (about 1 cup of water)
A little extra flour for opening the pie
2 cup flour for all uses (or mixed with whole flour)
Water as long as it takes (about 1 cup of water)
A little extra flour for opening the pie

instructions: Arab pies become unleavened. The unleavened dough gets thinner. With minimal yeast in the dough, the Arabic pie becomes more fluffy and soft.
In a bowl, add the flour, yeast, salt, sugar and water.
Mix all the ingredients manually or with the mixer until a malleable soft dough is formed.
The dough will lead you if you need some extra flour or little water.
Remove the dough on the counter and ferment for a few minutes to become elastic and smooth.
Pour a few drops of oil into a bowl.
Put the dough in, cover it with a kitchen membrane or wet cloth.
Allow the dough for Arab pie to rest for 30 minutes.
Divide the dough into six pieces. We'll make six Arab pies.
Form six dough balls. We touch them on a floured surface always with the compound underneath.
Press the dough balls to spread it slightly.
Cover them with towel and let them stand there and rest for 10’.
On a floured surface, open a leaf with the hands wide. With the stoner we open into thin Arabic pie.
Whenever necessary, sprinkle with a minimum flour the working surface so that the dough does not stick.
We take each pie lightly with the fingers like when we open pizza.
In a bowl, add the yeast, sugar and two tablespoons of water.
Cover with membrane and allow 5-10 minutes to activate the yeast and show bubbles on the surface.
Mix all the ingredients in a bowl until a malleable soft dough is formed.
Ferment our dough on the counter to make it elastic and smooth.
Cover with membrane and allow it to stand from 40 minutes to an hour and rise.
Divide our dough into six pieces and on a floury surface, open one into thin Arabic pie.
In a hot anti-stick vessel without oily substance, bake every pie for a minute on each side, always on medium heat.
Fill Arab pies with ingredients of our liking, such as chicken, tomato, onion, peppers, tzatziki or yoghurt sauce.

arabic-pie-with-chicken-argiro.name: Arabic Pie With Chicken

ingredients.item: 2 cup of hard flour for bread
A little flour for opening pie
2 cup of hard flour for bread
A little flour for opening pie

instructions: Put the chicken first between film or parchment paper and beat it to become thin.
Mix the marinade ingredients in a bowl and dip the chicken.
Cover and allow to marinate at least for 30′′.
Cut into thin slices.
Heat the olive oil and add the onion and peppers.
Leave for 5′ at high heat.
Add the chicken, mix and bake for 5′.
Remove from heat, add parsley and yogurt and sprinkle paprika or pepper.
Wrap it in suvlaki with Arab pie.

arabic-pie-with-salmon-argiro.name: Arabic Pie With Salmon

ingredients.item: 1 large carrot grated on thick grater
1 large carrot grated on thick grater

instructions: In a bowl, add the finock, carrot, cabbage, dill, juice and yogurt.
Add salt and pepper and mix well.
We open the Arab pies on our bench and we equalise the salmon and vegetable salad we prepared on each of them.
Then we wrap them up like the skewer.
Wrap them in membrane to keep their shape in the refrigerator or eat them immediately.
See here my recipe for making homemade Arabic pies with your hands!

arabic-pies-with-3-ingredients-argiro.name: Arabic Pies With 3 Ingredients

ingredients.item: Water as long as it takes (about 2/3 of the cup)
Water as long as it takes (about 2/3 of the cup)

instructions: Arab yeast-free pie is an unleavened dough that means a dough without any swelling.
The use of the yeast will give a little more fluffy dough.
Add the flour and salt to a bowl.
Mix with a spoon.
Add the water and ferment.
Once the dough's in a ball, we put it on the work bench.
Bake for 5’ until the dough is smooth and does not stick in the hands.
Place the dough in a bowl.
Cover it and let it stand for 30 minutes to rest.
Divide the dough into 6 pieces.
Open each piece of dough in thin sheet with the molder.
Add minimum flour to the work bench whenever necessary so that the pie does not stick.
Heat a large non-stick pan over medium heat.
Touch 1-1 pie in the hot pan.
Bake without oil for 2’ and turn over.
Bake from the other side
We make the rest.

arabic-red-lentil-soup-pequerecetas.name: Arabic Red Lentil Soup

ingredients.item: 800 ml de agua o caldo de verdura

instructions: We washed the red lentils well in a paddle under the tap till the water came clear and we scattered. We heated olive oil into a pot with medium heat. We added the chopped onion and pitted it for a few minutes, stirring from time to time, till it's soft and clear. We put the chopped garlic and we briefly soften it, without it getting golden.
We add the cumin and turmeric and remove for a few seconds so that the spices are integrated.
We added the red lentils and water or broth. Let's remove and purify and remove the foam if necessary.
We reduce fire and let them cook with soft heat for about 15- 20 minutes, stirring from time to time, till lentils are completely tender.
Retiramos la olla del fuego y trituramos las lentejas con una batidora hasta obtener una textura fina y cremosa.
We adjusted salt and added lemon juice to taste just before serving. Let's have a good drink and serve hot.

arabica-argiro.name: Arabica
instructions: Boil the pens in salted water for 2 minutes less than the time on the pack.
In a pot heat the olive oil and sauté the garlic with the bouboubouvo for half a minute, over medium heat.
Add the paste and rub it in the pot until it removes its flavors.
Add the tomatoes, melt them with the spoon and let the sauce boil for 10 minutes.
Salt at the end and add the pens along with some of their water.
Continue boiling for 2 minutes so that the sauce thickens.
Remove from heat, add fresh basil and serve immediately.
Sprinkle with grated cheese and sprinkle with some olive oil.

aragonese-cookies-teleculinaria.name: Aragonese Cookies
instructions: Put on a tray with butter and book it. In a bowl, crush the butter with the sugar and a pinch of salt. Then add the eggs and then the flour, always kneading. Finally, add the rolled almond and wrap.
Lay the previous preparation on the tray, smooth and put it in the oven, preheated at 180oC, for 15 minutes. Remove from the oven, deform and let it cool.
Depois de fria, corte a massa em fatias com uma faca de serrilha. Leve-as a tostar em forno bem quente. Retire, deixe arrefecer e sirva.

arancini-argiro.name: Arancini
instructions: If we have risotto left over, make the arancinoni, fill with all the previous ingredients, bake in a gallet and egg and fry for a few minutes 7-8 minutes until golden.
Otherwise make risotto from the beginning following the following procedure:
Heat the oil and butter in a deep pan when it burns, add the onion and sauté for a few minutes to become transparent.
Add the garlic and sauté 1′.
Add the rice and mix 1’-2′ to oil and become transparent.
Add the wine and let it evaporate almost all. Start adding a spoonful of hot broth until it drinks it each time.
Each time the rice sucks the previous broth, add the next dose until the rice boils. Salt and pepper. This process lasts 20-25′ and in the end we drop the parmesan.
After the risotto is done, pour it into a baking pan and let it cool completely. Read here all the secrets about the perfect risotto.
After the risotto cools, we take rice balls like a big nut and fill them with a piece of cheese.
Create round riceballs, set aside until the whole rice is finished.
We make a total of 20 arachinis.
Add the flour, beaten eggs and grated toast to 3 different bowls.
Dip each arantini first in the flour, shake, dip in the egg, and finish on the toast.
Leave the rice cakes in order until all of them are ready.
Heat oil for frying in a pot with a thick base. The oil should cover the riceballs above the waist.
The power of fire must be moderate, not strong.
Once the oil is burned, add a few aracini and fry until golden-roded. About 8′ in total.
We put them with a drill spoon on a platter with absorbent paper to drain.
We enjoy them warm because they are irresistible. Don't let them freeze. They'll turn into rubber.
Serve them with a tomato sauce for dips to the end.

arancini-italian-rice-balls-epicurious.name: Arancini (italian Rice Balls) | Epicurious
instructions: Combine the rice and broth in a large pot. Bring to a boil and then reduce to a simmer, cover, and cook until the water is absorbed and the rice is soft and creamy, about 15 to 20 minutes. Stir occasionally to ensure the rice doesn’t stick to the bottom of the pot.
While the rice is cooking, heat the butter and olive oil in a small pan over medium heat and add the prosciutto. Cook until it begins to crisp, about 2 minutes. Set aside.
Once the rice is cooked, remove to a large baking sheet and let cool for 10 minutes. (Rice can be made a day ahead, cooled, and stored in the refrigerator overnight.)
While the rice is cooling, pour 1/2 cup (120 ml) room-temperature water into a shallow bowl. Place the bread crumbs in a separate shallow bowl. Set both aside.
Once the rice is cool, combine it with the crisped prosciutto, egg, parsley, Parmigiano cheese, and salt in a large bowl.

Using an ice cream scoop or lightly wet hands, create a 2-inch (5-cm) round ball with the rice mixture. Make an indentation in the middle and add a cube of mozzarella cheese. Encase the cheese with rice and reshape into a perfect ball, then set on the baking sheet. Repeat with the remaining rice mixture.
Using your hands, lightly wet the outside of each of the rice balls with the room-temperature water, then roll in the bread crumbs. Set them on the baking sheet.

Heat the vegetable oil in a large pot to 177°C (175°C). Drop 4 rice balls into the oil (they should fully submerge) and cook until the outside is golden brown, about 3 minutes. If your pot is wide, carefully roll the rice balls in the oil to ensure even cooking on all sides.
Transfer to paper towels to drain. Repeat with the remaining rice balls. They are best eaten at room temperature, garnished with a sprinkling of grated Parmesan cheese, if you can wait!

arancini-stuffed-italian-rice-balls.name: Arancini (stuffed Italian Rice Balls)

ingredients.item: . chopped oil-packed calabrian chiles from a jar
Low-sodium homemade chicken stock or store-bought broth
Diamond crystal or 1 tsp. morton kosher salt

instructions:
Mix 3 Tbsp. chopped oil-packed Calabrian chiles from a jar, 2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil, 1½ tsp. finely grated lemon zest, 2 tsp. fresh lemon juice, and ½ small garlic clove, finely grated, in a small bowl to combine.
Do Ahead: Sauce can be made 12 hours ahead. Cover tightly with plastic wrap and chill.
Bring 3½ cups low-sodium homemade chicken stock or store-bought broth to a simmer in a medium pot over medium heat, then keep warm over low.

Heat 2 Tbsp. unsalted butter in a medium saucepan over medium. Add 1 medium onion, finely chopped, and cook, stirring often, until softened but not browned, 4–5 minutes. Add 2 garlic cloves, thinly sliced, and cook, stirring constantly, until softened but not browned, about 1 more minute.

Stir in 1 cup carnaroli rice or arborio rice; season with 1½ tsp. Diamond Crystal or 1 tsp. Morton kosher salt. Cook, stirring often, until some of the grains turn translucent around the edges, about 3 minutes. Add ½ cup dry white wine and cook, stirring often, until pan is almost dry, about 2 minutes. Ladle in 1 cup warm stock, bring to a simmer, and cook, stirring often, until liquid is absorbed, about 4 minutes. Ladle in another 1 cup stock and continue to cook, stirring yet again, until most of the liquid is absorbed, 5–7 minutes. Add remaining 1½ cups stock and cook, stirring often, until rice is cooked through but retains some bite and liquid is mostly absorbed, 9–11 minutes longer. Remove from heat. Stir in 2 oz. finely grated Parmesan cheese (about 1 cup), ¼ cup heavy cream, 2 tsp. finely grated lemon zest, 1 Tbsp. fresh lemon juice, 1 tsp. freshly ground black pepper, and remaining 2 Tbsp. unsalted butter. Season risotto with kosher salt.

Spread risotto in an even layer on a rimmed, parchment-lined baking sheet. Refrigerate at least 1 hour.
Do Ahead: Risotto can be prepared and chilled 24 hours in advance. Cover with plastic wrap after 1 hour so it doesn’t develop a skin.

Line another rimmed baking sheet with parchment paper. Scoop about ¼ cup risotto into your hands and form into a patty about 2½" in diameter (it doesn’t need to be perfect!). Place 2–3 pieces mozzarella from 3 oz. low-moisture mozzarella cheese, cut into ⅓" pieces, in the center of patty. Carefully pinch and shape risotto so it completely encases the cheese, then roll into a 2" ball. Your hands might get messy, and the balls may be slightly misshapen (they’ll firm up later). Place on prepared baking sheet. Repeat with remaining risotto and mozzarella. Freeze rice balls 10 minutes.

Meanwhile, pulse 2 cups panko in a food processor or place in a ziptop plastic bag and crush with a rolling pin until finer crumbs form. Transfer to a shallow bowl. Place ½ cup all-purpose flour in another shallow bowl. Lightly beat 2 large eggs in a third shallow bowl to blend; season all bowls with kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper. Working one at a time, dredge rice balls in flour, shaking off excess. Transfer to bowl with egg and turn to coat, letting excess drip back into bowl. Coat with panko, pressing gently to adhere. Transfer to a parchment-lined rimmed baking sheet. Chill rice balls while you heat oil.

Pour canola or vegetable oil (about 6 cups) into a medium heavy pot fitted with thermometer to a depth of 2". Warm oil over medium-high heat until thermometer registers 350°. Carefully lower half of rice balls into oil with a slotted spoon or spider and fry until deeply golden brown, 6–8 minutes. Transfer to paper towels to drain; season with salt. Repeat with remaining batch of rice balls. Serve warm with sauce alongside. 

Editor’s note: This arancini recipe was first printed November 2018. Head this way for more of our best Italian recipes →

Sade sevenler sadece peynir ve biraz bezelye ekleyebilirler.

You can use a small amount of muskat rende of more dense flavor.
You can use panko bread crumb instead of galeta; it becomes more ç

instructions: 1. Onion and yellowms roast.
2. Add Pirinci, stir up to transparent.
3. Sebze/tavuk suyunu ekle, çektir.
4. Cook the risottoyu cream until it comes to consistency by adding the remaining hot water azar azar.
5. Mix and cool with Parmesan.
6. Stirring risottodan parç take, cheese or i&ccedil in the middle; harç bay, round.
7. Un,   bull and fry and serve the galeta.
8.  Afiyet olsun.

aranitas-shredded-green-plantain-patties.name: Aranitas (shredded Green Plantain Patties)
instructions: Heat canola oil in a deep-fryer or large saucepan to 191°C (190 degrees C). Gather about 2 tablespoons of shredded plantains and shape into patties.
Fry the patties in the preheated oil until golden and crispy, about 10 minutes. Drain on paper towels and season with garlic salt.

archangel.name: Archangel
instructions: Muddle 2 cucumber slices, ¼" thick in a mixing glass to break up cucumber and release juice.
Add 2¼ oz. gin and ¾ oz. Aperol, then fill with ice.
Stir for 25 seconds—don’t rush it; dilution is crucial here.
Double-strain, using a cocktail strainer over the mixing glass and a fine-mesh strainer, into a cocktail glass and garnish with a lemon twist.

arctic-char-gravlax-with-white-grapefrui.name: Arctic Char Gravlax With White Grapefruit

ingredients.item: Plus 2 tsp. finely grated white or pink grapefruit zest
Muscovado sugar or light brown sugar
Plus 1 tsp. dried whole green peppercorns

instructions: Place arctic char skin side down on a large piece of plastic wrap. Mix 3 Tbsp. grapefruit zest, 3 Tbsp. salt, both sugars, 1 Tbsp. crushed green peppercorns, and crushed juniper berries in a small bowl; sprinkle mixture over fish, spreading evenly and pressing gently so spices adhere. Wrap plastic tightly around fish, then wrap with another large sheet of plastic. Gently poke 24 small holes through plastic (not fish) on both sides of fish with a thin skewer or the tip of a sharp knife to allow juices to escape. Put fish on a rimmed baking sheet. Top with another rimmed baking sheet; weigh it down with two 15-oz. canned goods. Refrigerate for 2 days, turning fish after 1 day.

Remove canned goods and top baking sheet. Unwrap and discard plastic, keeping cure intact. Rewrap in clean plastic and poke holes in plastic as before. Place in a clean resealable plastic bag; refrigerate, skin side up. Chill for 1 more day. Gently scrape off cure.

Whisk crème fraîche, 2 tsp. dill, and remaining 1 tsp. crushed green peppercorns in a small bowl. Season with salt. Thinly slice gravlax on a diagonal and serve on crackers. Top with créme fraîche and garnish with re-maining 2 tsp. grapefruit zest and 1 tsp. dill.

arctic-char-tostadas.name: Arctic Char Tostadas

ingredients.item: -oz. boneless arctic char or salmon fillet

ingredients.prep: sliced serrano chiles, capers, and lime wedges (for serving)

instructions: Heat a small saucepan over medium. When hot, pour in 1 Tbsp. fish sauce. It should bubble up vigorously and then get thicker and slightly darker, about 30 seconds. Repeat with remaining fish sauce, incorporating 1 Tbsp. at a time. Carefully add vinegar (it may spatter), then transfer mixture to a small bowl; let cool. Stir in scallions and ginger. Taste and season with salt.
Do Ahead: Vinaigrette can be made 1 week ahead. Cover and chill.

Preheat oven to 300°. Mix lemon zest into cheese in a small bowl; season with salt and set aside. Place fish on a rimmed baking sheet and drizzle very lightly with oil; season with salt. Bake until just opaque throughout, about 5 minutes for char and 8–10 minutes for salmon. Remove from oven and break apart into large flakes. Drizzle with ¼ cup Salty-Sour Vinaigrette.
Divide reserved cheese mixture among tostadas. Top with fish, then cilantro, chiles, and capers. Serve with lime wedges.

arctic-char-with-cucumber-feta-relish-an.name: Arctic Char With Cucumber-feta Relish And Jalapeño-goat-cheese Hush Puppies

ingredients.item: - to 6-ounce arctic char fillets with skin

instructions: Toss first 7 ingredients in medium bowl. Season relish with salt and freshly ground black pepper. DO AHEAD Can be made 2 hours ahead. Cover and chill.

Sprinkle fish on both sides with salt and freshly ground pepper. Heat oil in heavy large skillet over medium-high heat. Place fish, skin side down, in skillet. Cook until skin is brown, occasionally flattening with spatula to prevent curling, about 4 minutes. Turn fish over. Cook until just opaque in center, about 1 minute.
Using slotted spoon, mound relish on plates. Top with fish, skin side up. Arrange hush puppies alongside and serve.

arctic-char-with-greens-and-gribiche-dre.name: Arctic Char With Greens And Gribiche Dressing
instructions: Season fish with salt and pepper. Swirl oil in a cold large nonstick skillet to coat; lay in fish in pan, skin side down. Set over medium heat and cook fish, occasionally pressing down lightly (lightly!) on fillets to ensure skin is in even contact with pan, until skin is deep golden brown and flesh is opaque around the edges, 6–8 minutes. Turn fillets and cook just until opaque on flesh side, about 1 minute longer. Transfer to a platter.
Gently toss greens in a medium bowl with enough dressing to lightly coat; season with salt and pepper. Scatter over fish and spoon some additional dressing on top.

arctic-char-with-king-trumpet-mushrooms-.name: Arctic Char With King Trumpet Mushrooms, Fingerlings, And Meyer Lemon Butter Sauce

ingredients.item: Leeks (white and pale green parts only)
ingredients.prep: halved lengthwise, cut crosswise into 1/4-inch-thick slices
cut crosswise into 1/4-inch-thick rounds or 1 pound oyster mushrooms

instructions: Melt butter in heavy medium skillet over medium-high heat. Add leeks and sauté until tender, about 8 minutes. Season to taste with salt and pepper. DO AHEAD Can be made 2 days ahead. Cover and refrigerate.
Puree chives and oil in blender. Strain through fine sieve. Season oil to taste with salt and pepper. DO AHEAD Can be made 1 day ahead. Cover and refrigerate. Bring to room temperature before using.

Bring large saucepan of salted water to boil; add thyme and bay leaf. Add potatoes and boil until tender, about 14 minutes. Drain. Cut into 1-inch pieces. Return to pan; add butter and toss to coat. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Set potatoes aside.
Melt butter in heavy large skillet over medium-high heat. Add mushrooms and sauté until tender, about 8 minutes. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Set aside.

Using small sharp knife, cut off peel and white pith from lemons. Working over small bowl to catch juices, cut between membranes to release segments. Add segments to juice in bowl. Bring cream to simmer in small skillet over medium heat. butter and whisk until melted. Mix in lemon segments and juice. Simmer until thick, whisking occasionally, about 2 minutes (segments will fall apart). Season to taste with salt and pepper. DO AHEAD Potatoes, mushrooms, and lemon butter sauce can be made 2 hours ahead. Let stand at room temperature.

Heat oil in heavy large skillet over high heat. Sprinkle fish with thyme, salt, and pepper. Add fish to skillet, skin side down, and cook until skin is brown, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat. Turn fish over. Place lid on skillet to hold fish; tilt skillet to drain off excess oil. Discard oil. Add butter to skillet and tilt to melt butter and distribute evenly. Let stand covered until fish is just opaque in center, about 3 minutes.

Rewarm leeks, potatoes, and mushrooms over medium heat and spoon onto plates; top with fish. Rewarm lemon butter sauce over medium heat, whisking frequently; spoon over fish. Drizzle with chive oil. Garnish with chervil and serve.

arctic-char-with-truffled-mashed-potatoe.name: Arctic Char With Truffled Mashed Potatoes, Sugar Snap Peas, And Truffle Vinaigrette

ingredients.item: Sliced black truffles from jar plus 1 teaspoon juice from jar*
Chopped sliced black truffles from jar
- to 7-ounce arctic char fillets with skin

instructions: Blend first 4 ingredients in mini processor until well blended. Gradually add both oils and blend until thick and creamy. Season to taste with salt and pepper. DO AHEAD Can be made 1 day ahead. Cover and chill. Let stand 1 hour at room temperature before serving.

Cook potatoes in pot of boiling salted water until tender, about 30 minutes. Drain potatoes. Cool slightly, then peel. Return potatoes to pot; add butter and mash. Mix in truffles and truffle oil, then enough milk by tablespoonfuls to moisten. DO AHEAD Can be made 2 hours ahead. Let stand at room temperature. Rewarm over low heat before serving.

Preheat oven to 149°C. Butter rimmed baking sheet. Arrange fish, skin side down, on prepared sheet. Sprinkle fish with salt, pepper, and chives. Bake fish until barely opaque in center, 10 to 15 minutes.

Meanwhile, melt butter in large skillet over medium heat. Add sugar snap peas and 2 tablespoons water; sauté until peas are crisp-tender and water evaporates, about 4 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.
Spoon mashed potatoes onto plates. Top with fish and sugar snap peas. Drizzle vinaigrette around fish and serve.

arepa-recipe-lezzet.name: Arepa Recipe

ingredients.item: 2 cups öpre cooked corn flour (masarepa – white or yellow)
1 tablespoon of liquid oil (optional, softness için)
1 tablespoon of butter (push after cheek flavor için– optional)
The traditional tablerepa (öpre cooked corn flour) or corn germ (cornmeal) or fine öğütülmü corn flour can be used. but endç slightly more different textured.
Glutensiz seçenek için pirinç unu veya kinoa unu eklenerek harmanlanabilir.
Flavor salts can be used, such as sea salt, garlic powder, onion powder instead of normal salt.
Add extra taste of Hamura can be added to için reindeed cheese (mozarella, Kasar, queso fresco).
It can be made more flavored and nutritious using warm süt or meat/ chicken water instead of water.
A mild sweet version için coconut sütü or almond sütü can be used.
Instead of liquid oil, olive oil, coconut oil or butter can be preferred.

Divide la masa en porciones iguales y dales forma de bolita.

Warm an iron or frying pan with medium-high heat. It cooks each arepa about 5 minutes on each side, until they are golden and form a slightly crispy bark.

Cooking chicken breast with salt water till tender. Let cool and crumbling. In a frying pan, heats up a tablespoon of oil and pits onion and garlic till they are clear. Add the pitted chicken and leap for a few minutes. Hail to taste.

Do four balls of the same size and clamp them with their palms and round them to form an arepas.
Sobre una plancha o budare caliente cuece las arepas hasta que tengan una capa dura en su exterior.
Then put them into the oven at 120 ° C, this process is just so they can be inflamed and filled more easily.
Once ready, have them put some rest and open them up.
Agrega en otro bol la harina de trigo, los huevos, la sal, la mostaza y la leche; revuelve con un tenedor hasta unificar la mezcla.
Agregar poco a poco el agua hasta obtener una mezcla espesa para el rebozado (solo debe colocarse el agua necesaria).
Add a slice of bordadela inside the arepa (it's important that the bordadela doesn't stand out from the arepa).
Cierra la arepa con la ayuda de palillos, sumerge la arepa en la mezcla del rebozado utilizando unas pinzas. Debe quedar cubierta por ambos lados.

Ponla sobre papel absorbente. Repite con todas las arepas.
Open and fill with hand cheese, cabbage, tomato tirites, mayonnaise, tomato sauce (ketchup) and mustard.
Sirve caliente para que se mantenga la textura crujiente.

arepas.name: Arepas

ingredients.item: Desired fillings (such as shredded cooked chicken or pork
ingredients.prep: stewed black beans with cheese and lime, corn salad with onion and fresh herbs; for serving)

ingredients.item: Arepa flour is precooked corn flour
ingredients.prep: not to be confused with masa harina. Sometimes sold as masarepa or harina precocida, it can be found in Latin markets and some supermarkets.

instructions: Combine arepa flour and salt in a medium bowl. Make a well in the center and add 2½ cups warm water. Using a wooden spoon, gradually incorporate dry ingredients, stirring until no dry lumps remain. Let rest 5 minutes to hydrate.
Knead dough a few times in bowl, then divide into 8 pieces. Roll each piece on work surface into a ball, then gently flatten to about ½” thick.

Heat 1 Tbsp. oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add 4 arepas, cover, and cook until golden brown, 6–8 minutes. Uncover, flip, and cook (keep uncovered) until other side is golden brown, 6–8 minutes. Transfer arepas to a wire rack. Repeat with remaining 1 Tbsp. oil and dough.
Split arepas and stuff with desired fillings; serve with lime wedges for squeezing over.

arepas-recipe-in-air-fryer-comedera.name: Arepas Recipe In Air Fryer
arepas-recipe-in-air-fryer-comedera.original_name: Receta de arepas en freidora de aire
instructions: Inicia la receta y calienta una olla con agua.
Luego, vierte el agua en un bol y sazona el agua con sal y mezcla hasta que tome sabor.
Fold early and with the help of a pallet or your hands, mix well till a soft mass forms.
Now, preheat the air fryer at 180 ° C
It moistens your hands and takes a lump of dough and forms the arepa with its light till it's round.
Coloca la arepa en el agua hirviendo y deja por 4 minutos. Posteriormente, retira del agua, escurre un poco y lleva a la freidora de aire.
Cooking from 7 to 10 minutes more at 160 ° C, till crispy and ready.
Disfrutas tus arepas como gustes.

arepas-with-black-beans-and-avocado.name: Arepas With Black Beans And Avocado

ingredients.item: . diamond crystal or 1 tsp. morton kosher salt

instructions: Preheat oven to 325°. Toast seeds on a parchment paper–lined rimmed baking sheet, tossing once, until darkened and fragrant, 8–10 minutes. Transfer to a small bowl; reserve baking sheet and parchment.

Meanwhile, combine flour and 2 tsp. Diamond Crystal or 1 tsp. Morton salt in a large bowl. Add 2 tsp. oil and 1 cup warm water and mix with a sturdy spatula or your hands to combine. If dough feels dry, add more water 1 tablespoonful at a time until it holds together. Smash and knead dough until smooth and no small clumps or dry bits remain (be patient and really knead it—it will hydrate and come together); it shouldn’t crack when you squeeze it. Cover bowl with a damp kitchen towel and set aside to let dough hydrate, 15 minutes.

Halve chile lengthwise, removing seeds and membranes if less heat is desired. Place one half in a food processor. Finely chop remaining chile and set aside. Add avocado, ½ cup cilantro, and juice from 1 lime to food processor and pulse until mostly smooth. With the motor running, drizzle in 2 Tbsp. oil until a smooth sauce forms. Taste and season with salt.

Add all but 2 Tbsp. toasted seeds to hydrated dough. Knead in bowl until seeds are incorporated and dough is firm and homogenous, 1–2 minutes. Turn out dough onto reserved parchment and divide into 4 pieces. Press and pat into disks that are 5"–5½" in diameter and ⅛"–¼" thick. Return disks with parchment to baking sheet.

Heat 1 tsp. oil in a large cast-iron or nonstick skillet over medium-high until shimmering. Working in batches, add arepas and cook, turning once with a spatula and reducing heat to medium if they’re browning too quickly, until a crisp, slightly charred crust forms, 3–5 minutes per side. Transfer arepas to same baking sheet and bake until slightly puffed and springy, 10–12 minutes.

While arepas bake, wipe out skillet, then pour in remaining 1 Tbsp. oil and heat over medium. Add chili powder and cook, stirring, until fragrant, about 20 seconds. Add beans and cook, stirring frequently, until warmed through, 3–4 minutes. Add reserved chopped chile and cook, stirring, until fragrant, about 1 more minute. Remove from heat and add remaining ½ cup cilantro. Season with salt.
Spread arepas with avocado sauce. Top with bean mixture, cheese (if using), and reserved seeds.

argentine-empanadas-with-turkey-meat-com.name: Argentine Empanadas With Turkey Meat
argentine-empanadas-with-turkey-meat-com.original_name: Empanadas argentinas con carne de pavo

ingredients.item: Two cdas. Unbone green olives, cut into sheets
2 cdas. aceite de girasol o de oliva

instructions: Descongela los discos de empanadas: ponlos a temperatura ambiente por al menos 20 minutos.

argentine-gaucha-sauce-and-delicious-com.name: Argentine Gaucha Sauce, And Delicious
argentine-gaucha-sauce-and-delicious-com.original_name: Salsa gaucha argentina, fácil y deliciosa
instructions: In a frying pan add the shredded natural tomato, the rosemary and the bay leaf and let us cook for about 20 minutes.
After cooking time removes the rosemary and the bay leaf and we remove the frying pan from the fire.
At the beaker's glass, pour the frying pan and add garlic, parsley, cumin, oregano, sweet pimenton, wine vinegar, some salt, some pepper and we shredded everything.
Incorpora el aceite de oliva, poco a poco, en forma de hilo.
Deja enfriar y disfruta con lo que te provoque.

argentinean-inspired-beef-mini-empanadas.name: Argentinean-inspired Beef Mini Empanadas

ingredients.item: Salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Prepare filling: Cook ground beef, onion, bell pepper, and garlic in a large, nonstick skillet over medium to medium-high heat until vegetables are tender and beef is browned and crumbly, about 10 minutes. Add olives, paprika, cumin, salt, and pepper. Stir in chopped eggs.
Scrape mixture into a bowl. Cover and refrigerate until fully chilled, 1 to 2 hours; this ensures the dough will not be soggy when the empanadas are baked.
Prepare dough: Whisk flour and salt together in a medium bowl until combined. Cut in butter with a pastry blender until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.

Whisk cold water, egg, and vinegar together in a small bowl. Pour into the dry ingredients and blend with a fork. Use your hands to finish blending the ingredients until they come together in a ball. Flatten the ball into a disc, wrap with plastic wrap, and refrigerate for 1 hour.
When you are ready to cook, preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C).
Roll the dough on a lightly floured surface. Use a 3-inch round cutter to cut circles, rerolling the scraps as necessary. I got about 18 circles.
Hold each circle in your hand and place a heaping tablespoon of filling in the center. Gently fold the ends over the top and press to seal all the way around. Reserve extra filling for another use.
Set empanadas on a baking sheet. Press around the sealed edges with a fork if desired.
Bake in the preheated oven until golden brown on the bottoms, about 18 minutes. Flip and bake until the other side is browned, about 5 more minutes.

argentinian-beef-empanadas.name: Argentinian Beef Empanadas

ingredients.item: (12 each) puff pastry dough for turnovers/empanadas

ingredients.prep: rinsed well, cut in half lengthwise, divided

instructions: Heat 2 Tbsp. oil in a large pot over high. Cook 2 lb. ground beef, breaking up with a spoon, until browned but not completely cooked through, 6–8 minutes. Transfer to a medium bowl with a slotted spoon, leaving as much fat in pan as possible.

Reduce heat to medium and cook 2 medium onions, chopped, 2 small red bell peppers, seeded, chopped, and remaining 1 Tbsp. oil, stirring, until tender but not browned, 6–8 minutes; season with salt and black pepper. Add 3 Tbsp. ground cumin, 2 Tbsp. sweet paprika, 1 Tbsp. dried oregano, and ¼ tsp. cayenne pepper and cook, stirring, until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add 1½ cups low-sodium chicken stock or broth and reserved beef along with any accumulated juices to pot. Stir in 2 tsp. sugar, 4 tsp. salt, and ¼ tsp. black pepper. Bring to a simmer and cook, stirring and scraping up any brown bits, until most of the liquid is evaporated, 15–20 minutes; taste and season with salt and black pepper, if needed. Stir in ½ cup raisins. Transfer to a medium bowl, cover, and chill at least 3 hours.
Do Ahead: Beef filling can be made 3 days ahead. Keep chilled.

Preheat oven to 375°. Let 3 packages Puff Pastry Dough for Empanadas sit at room temperature 15 minutes to temper. Remove 6 rounds from package, keeping plastic divider underneath, and arrange on a work surface. Place 2 Tbsp. filling in the center of each round.
Top with 2 olive halves. Brush water around half of outer edge of each round.
Using plastic divider to help you, fold round over filling and pinch edges to seal.
Using a fork, crimp edges. Remove plastic and transfer empanada to a parchment-lined sheet tray, spacing 1" apart. Repeat with remaining rounds (you’ll get about 12 empanadas on each tray).

Bake empanadas, rotating tray halfway through, until golden brown and slightly darker around the edges, 25–35 minutes.
Do Ahead: Unbaked empanadas can be made 3 months ahead; freeze on sheet tray, then transfer to freezer bags and keep frozen.

argentinian-chicken-empanadas.name: Argentinian Chicken Empanadas

ingredients.item: (12 each) puff pastry dough for turnovers/empanadas

instructions: Bring chicken, bay leaves, and 2½ cups water to a simmer in a medium pot over medium and cook until chicken is cooked through, 30–40 minutes. Transfer chicken to a plate; discard bay leaves and reserve broth. Let chicken sit until cool enough to handle, then coarsely chop.

Meanwhile, heat oil in a large pot over medium and cook onion and bell peppers, stirring, until tender but not browned, 6–8 minutes; season with salt and black pepper. Stir in tomato paste and cook until brick red, 1–2 minutes. Add paprika, oregano, and cayenne and cook, stirring, until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add reserved broth and chicken along with any accumulated juices to pot. Stir in 4 tsp. salt and ¼ tsp. black pepper. Bring to a simmer and cook, stirring and scraping up any brown bits, until most of the liquid is evaporated, 15–20 minutes; taste and season with salt and black pepper, if needed. Transfer to a medium bowl, cover, and chill at least 3 hours.

Preheat oven to 375°. Let dough sit at room temperature 15 minutes to temper. Remove 6 rounds from package, keeping plastic divider underneath, and arrange on a work surface. Place 2 Tbsp. filling in the center of each round. Brush water around half of outer edge of each round. Using plastic divider to help you, fold round over filling and pinch edges to seal. Using a fork, crimp edges. Remove plastic and transfer empanada to a parchment-lined sheet tray, spacing 1" apart. Repeat with remaining rounds (you’ll get about 12 empanadas on each tray).
Bake empanadas, rotating tray halfway through, until golden brown and slightly darker around the edges, 25–35 minutes.
Do Ahead: Filling can be made 3 days ahead. Keep chilled. Unbaked empanadas can be made 3 months ahead; freeze on sheet tray, then transfer to freezer bags and keep frozen.

argyros-giant-salad-argiro.name: Argyros Giant Salad
instructions: Start with the soaking in plenty of water with soda (it takes at least 12 hours).
The next day rinse and boil for 45 minutes to soften.
Drain and place them in a salad box.
Finely chop with a knife the onions, the Florine peppers and add them to the salad, along with the onion rings, the olive trees or the chopped herbs.
In lemon juice dissolve salt with fork or wire.
Add the pepper and mustard and mix.
Add the olive oil and beat until the sauce turns into cream.
Pour, mix and serve this fantastic salad with giants!

argyros-milk-pie-argiro.name: Argyros Milk Pie
instructions: Butter and flour a round pan 30cm or 23×35cm diameter.
Mix flour with semolina in a bowl and set aside.
In a pot with a thick base, add 1 litre of milk and keep the rest separate.
Add the sugar, cinnamon, zest, vanilla and salt and let it warm over medium heat.
In a bowl, add the eggs and mix them with the wire.
Add the remaining milk, flour and semolina and continue mixing.
When the ingredients are combined, take the hot milk with a spoon and mix to heat the egg mixture.
Add another warm milk and mix and add the diluted mixture to the pot with the milk.
Mix with wire for a few minutes until the cream thickens and withdraw as soon as it boils.
Add the butter into the cream and mix until it melts.
Empty the buttered pan.
We straighten the surface by shaking the pan.

argyros-orange-liqueur-with-cinnamon-arg.name: Argyros Orange Liqueur With Cinnamon
argyros-orange-liqueur-with-cinnamon-arg.original_name: Λικέρ πορτοκάλι με κανέλα της Αργυρώς

ingredients.item: 1 sterilised large jar that closes airtightly
1 sterilised large jar that closes airtightly

instructions: Wash the oranges well first and with a sharp knife remove only the outer part of the peel (only yellow). The skin should be as thin as possible. If we leave the white place, it will make the orange liqueur bitter!
Place the peels in a bowl. Sprinkle them with 2-3 tablespoons of total sugar and squeeze them with your hands, with a slight wilting.
Put them in the sterile jar and pour in the cognac and vodka. Seal the jar and let it stand closed for 10 days.
Add the water and sugar to a pot and mix until it boils. Boil for 2'-3'. Allow the syrup to cool and add cold to the peel jar.
We throw the cinnamon. Seal the jar and allow the liqueur to mature for another week.
Drain the liqueur in a tulpan or a coffee filter. Put it in bottles and serve it cold.
Maturation time: 3 weeks
Find here all the instructions for use for citrus fruits.

aries-tea-blend.name: Aries Tea Blend
instructions: In a medium bowl, combine the black tea leaves, dried orange peel, and cinnamon. Mix well.
Store in an airtight container for up to 1 month.
To brew, steep 1-2 teaspoons of the tea blend in 8 ounces (240ml) of hot water for 5 minutes. Serve warm or over ice.
Enjoy!

arkansas-fried-veggie-skillet.name: Arkansas Fried Veggie Skillet

ingredients.prep: sliced into rings, or more to taste

ingredients.item: Salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Whisk milk and egg together in a large bowl. Pour cornmeal into another large bowl.
Dip squash, potatoes, tomatoes, okra, onion, and green bell pepper into milk mixture; press vegetables into cornmeal to coat.
Heat bacon grease in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook and stir vegetable mixture in hot grease until golden and tender, 15 to 25 minutes. Season with salt and black pepper.

armenian-stuffed-eggplant-imam-bayildi.name: Armenian Stuffed Eggplant (imam Bayildi)

ingredients.item: Tomato sauce with no salt added

instructions: Preheat the oven to 232°C (230 degrees C).
Cut eggplants in half lengthwise, then cut each half into thirds, making 12 eggplant spears. Place on a baking sheet; brush with 2 tablespoons olive oil.
Cook in the preheated oven until fork-tender, 15 to 20 minutes. Reduce the oven temperature to 177°C (175 degrees C).

Meanwhile, heat remaining 2 tablespoons olive oil in a medium to large saucepan over medium heat. Add bell pepper, onion, and garlic: cook until onion becomes tender, 5 to 7 minutes. Stir in chopped tomatoes; cook until most of the liquid has evaporated, 2 to 3 minutes. Set aside to cool.
Remove eggplants from the oven; cool slightly, 5 to 10 minutes. Transfer eggplants to a 9x13-inch baking dish.

Slice each spear vertically using a knife and fork, being careful not to cut through the skin. Pry each pocket open; fill with 2 to 3 tablespoons tomato filling. Cover filled eggplant spears with tomato sauce.
Bake in the preheated oven until bubbling, about 30 minutes. Season with salt and black pepper; garnish with parsley.

armenian-style-rice-pilaf.name: Armenian-style Rice Pilaf

ingredients.item: (2 ounces; 57g) thin pasta (such as vermicelli or fine spaghetti)
ingredients.prep: broken into 1-inch pieces, or 1/2 cup of small, non-tubular pasta, like orzo

ingredients.item: Homemade or store-bought low-sodium chicken stock
Diamond crystal kosher salt; if using table salt use half as much by volume or same weight
Minced fresh parsley or dill (or a combination)

instructions: Place rice in a medium bowl and rinse with hot tap water until water runs clear, about 30 seconds. Cover rice completely with fresh hot tap water and set aside for 10 minutes. Drain rice thoroughly in a fine-mesh strainer; discard soaking water.

Melt butter in a 3-quart saucepan over medium heat. Add the pasta and cook, stirring regularly, until evenly golden brown, 4 to 6 minutes. Add rice and cook, stirring occasionally, until edges of rice begin to turn translucent, about 3 minutes. Add stock, salt, and pepper, increase heat to high, and bring to boil. Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook until liquid is absorbed, about 10 minutes.

Remove pan from heat, remove the lid, cover pot with a folded dish towel, and set lid back in place. Let sit for 10 minutes. Fluff rice with a fork, stir in half of parsley, if using, and transfer to a serving dish. Top with remaining parsley and serve.

armenoville-ice-cream-cake-argiro.name: Armenoville (ice Cream Cake)

ingredients.item: 500 ml cream beaten in thick form
500 ml cream beaten in thick form

instructions: For the ice cream cake, place the herbal whipped cream in a bowl first and with the hand mixer beat it for 5 to 6 minutes hard, until soft peaks are formed.
Add the sugary milk and vanilla and continue the hit for 3 to 4 minutes.
Add the thick cream and gently with spatula mix the mixture.
Add the grated chocolate, crocan almonds and beiges broken.
We keep some crocan almonds and some beiges for the garnish.
Mix the mixture with spatula well.
We'll use a round form of 22 cm or a long cake.
We're dressing the form with membrane.
Empty the mixture.
Freeze for one night in the freezer.
Disforme, turn the Armenianville into a tour and remove the membrane.
Garnish with the crocan almonds (we kept) and the melted chocolate.
Around the platter we place broken beiges.

arnies-gimlet-slush.name: Arnie’s Gimlet Slush
instructions: Combine vodka, simple syrup, tea, and lime juice in a blender and freeze 3–12 hours (mixture will be partially frozen).
Add 2 cups ice to mixture and blend until thick and smooth. Divide among glasses and garnish with mint sprigs.

aromatic-basil-oil-with-garlic-argiro.name: Aromatic Basil Oil With Garlic
instructions: To make the aromatic basil oil with garlic, it is important first to wash and dry the basil very well.
We're chopping him up.
We'll need a sterile jar or a bottle with a thick neck.
Add the garlic, peppers, basil and peppers.
Then cover with the olive oil and seal the bottle.
Keep it in a dark and cool place or in the refrigerator, for at least 2 weeks, to perfume well.

aromatic-gingerbread-recipe-comedera.name: Aromatic Gingerbread Recipe
aromatic-gingerbread-recipe-comedera.original_name: Receta de pan de jengibre aromático
instructions: Drop the butter and then you'll add them to the cane molasses and mix them very well. Reserve.
In a big bowl, you'll add the flour, ginger, cinnamon and baking powder, mix so that everything's integrated.
Take your mixer and you'll beat your eggs by the sugar till you get a creamy texture.
Make a space in the middle of your dry, volcano-type ingredients to add mixed molasses with butter, eggs with sugar, water and vanilla. Also add the scented hazelnuts.
With your spatula's help, you're gonna mix up all the ingredients very well. You'll have a dense mass, but a little sticky, it'll be normal, that's texture.
Gather your oven at 180 degrees Celsius.
Fold your mix into the previously greased mold, and give them some knapshots so that everything will settle.
Hornea for about 45 minutes. When time's up, you'll do the stick test. If she gets wet, you have to leave her more minutes, otherwise she'll be more than ready.
Let him get cold so you can slice and taste it.

aromatic-orange-pudding-teleculinaria.name: Aromatic Orange Pudding
instructions: Bring 180 g of sugar to the fire and let it boil until you get a caramel. Lay it in one shape and caramelize it. Reserve.
Coloque num tacho o tomilho, o leite e a raspa de laranja. Leve ao lume e deixe levantar fervura. Baixe o lume e deixe ferver por mais 2 minutos. Retire do lume e passe por um coador.
In a bowl, beat the eggs and yolks with the remaining sugar. Add the orange juice and stir well. Finally, add the flavoured milk and lie in caramelized form.
Place the shape in the oven, preheated at 165oC, in sea bath, for 1 hour. Let it cool down completely before deforming. Serve fresh.

aromatic-pork-and-noodle-soup.name: Aromatic Pork And Noodle Soup

ingredients.item: Chiles de árbol or  0.5 tsp. crushed red pepper flakes
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper

ingredients.prep: fresh cilantro leaves with tender stems, thinly sliced scallions, chopped unsalted, roasted peanuts, and lime wedges (for serving)

instructions: Heat a dry large cast-iron skillet over medium-high. Cook onion, cut side down, until lightly charred, about 5 minutes; transfer to a plate.

Add chiles (if using crushed red pepper flakes, add with fennel seeds), garlic, cinnamon stick, and star anise to skillet and cook, stirring, until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add fennel seeds and cook, stirring, until fragrant, about 20 seconds (do not burn). Quickly transfer to a large saucepan (reserve skillet) and add onion, ginger, and broth; bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer until broth is flavorful.

Meanwhile, heat oil in reserved skillet over medium-high heat. Season pork with salt and pepper and cook until browned and cooked through, about 4 minutes per side. Transfer to a cutting board and let rest 5 minutes before thinly slicing.
Cook noodles according to package directions. Divide among bowls; add pork. Strain broth and ladle into bowls. Top soup with bean sprouts, cilantro, scallions, and peanuts and serve with lime wedges.

aromatic-puff-pastry-tart-with-pumpkin-a.name: Aromatic Puff Pastry Tart With Pumpkin And Carrot
aromatic-puff-pastry-tart-with-pumpkin-a.original_name: Αρωματική τάρτα σφολιάτας με κολοκύθι και καρότο

ingredients.item: 1 egg, split into yolk and white
1 egg, split into yolk and white

instructions: Wash and dry the vegetables.
Using the peeler (piller) or mandolin, cut into very thin almost transparent-long slices the pumpkin and carrot.
Add the thin slices of vegetables to a bowl, add the lemon zest and juice, olive oil, garlic, some salt and pepper and mix to oil well.
Let them marinate while preparing the base of the tart.
In a bowl, mix the cream cheese (or anthotyr), with feta cheese, yoghurt, yolk, mint and some pepper until we have a mixture of creamy texture.
Spread the puff pastry sheet on a shallow baking pan lined with non-stick paper. With a knife, cut a box of 21⁄2 cm to create a brush around the leaf.
Spread with a spoon on the base of the puff pastry the cheese mixture into a thin layer leaving the box we carved empty.
We rub the capillary perimeter with the egg white.
Add the vegetables over a layer covering the cheese mixture completely.
Bake the tart in a well preheated oven, at 200°C, in the air, in the medium grill, for 10 minutes.
Lower with the temperature at 180°C, sprinkle the surface of the tart with a cephalogravier and continue baking for 25 more minutes until the sfoliata is round and evenly browned.
Dilute the pesto with a few drops of olive oil and a few drops of lemon juice to make it more liquid and sprinkle the tart.
Let it stand for 5 minutes and drop its temperature and then cut and serve.

aromatic-red-rice.name: Aromatic Red Rice
instructions: In a small saucepan, bring rice, a pinch of salt, and 2 cups water to a boil.
Stick cloves into onion halves and add to pot along with chile.
Cover, reduce heat to a simmer, and cook until rice is tender, 30–40 minutes.
Let stand off heat, covered, 5 minutes.
Fluff with a fork before serving.

aromatic-rice-pilaf.name: Aromatic Rice Pilaf

ingredients.item: . diamond crystal or  0.5 tsp. morton kosher salt
Chopped tender herbs (such as parsley or cilantro) and lemon wedges

instructions: Whisk together 2 tsp. store-bought fried onions, 1 tsp. ground cumin, ½ tsp. ground cinnamon, ⅛ tsp. ground cardamom, and ⅛ tsp. ground cloves in a small bowl.

Melt 3 Tbsp. unsalted butter in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Add seasoning blend and cook, stirring often, until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add 1 cup jasmine rice, rinsed until water runs clear, and cook, stirring often and reducing heat if needed to keep spices from scorching, until grains are coated and opaque around the edges, about 2 minutes.

Pour in 1½ cups water and add 1 tsp. Diamond Crystal or ½ tsp. Morton kosher salt; stir to combine. Increase heat to medium-high and bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and cover saucepan with a tight-fitting lid. Cook until water is completely absorbed and rice is tender, 15–18 minutes. Remove pot from heat and let rice sit (still covered) 5–10 minutes.
Uncover rice and fluff gently with a fork. Transfer pilaf to a platter and top with chopped tender herbs (such as parsley or cilantro) if desired. Serve with lemon wedges if desired.

aromatic-shrimp-and-noodle-medicine-soup.name: Aromatic Shrimp And Noodle Medicine Soup

ingredients.prep: tough outer layer removed, lightly smashed
tough outer layer removed, finely chopped

ingredients.item: Fresh tender herbs (such as thai basil

instructions: Toast cloves, cardamom, star anise, chiles, cinnamon, and peppercorns in a large pot over medium heat, tossing often, until fragrant, about 2 minutes. Add cilantro, shallots, garlic, lemongrass, ginger, broth, and fish sauce and bring to a boil. Reduce heat; simmer until flavors meld, 1–1 ½ hours. Strain into a large bowl, pressing on solids. Return broth to pot; season with salt and pepper.

Pulse cashews in a food processor until very finely ground. Add shallot, chile, lemongrass, ginger, brown sugar, and shrimp paste; process until smooth. Heat oil in a small skillet over medium and cook paste, stirring, until beginning to brown, about 2 minutes.

Cook noodles according to package directions. Drain; rinse under cold water. Bring broth to a simmer, add shrimp, and cook until cooked through, about 2 minutes. Divide noodles among bowls. Add 2 Tbsp. paste to each and ladle broth over; stir to incorporate paste. Top with shrimp, sprouts, scallions, and herbs; season with pepper. Serve with chili oil and lime wedges alongside.

aromatic-white-rice.name: Aromatic White Rice

ingredients.item: . diamond crystal or  0.5 tsp. morton kosher salt

instructions: Heat 3 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil in a heavy medium saucepan over medium. Add 1 medium onion, finely chopped, 3 garlic cloves, finely chopped, and 1 tsp. Diamond Crystal or ½ tsp. Morton kosher salt. Cook, stirring often, until onion is softened, 5–7 minutes. Add 1 bay leaf and 3 cups long-grain white rice, rinsed; cook, stirring, until grains are coated in oil, about 30 seconds.

Pour in 4½ cups low-sodium chicken broth and stir once. Bring to a boil, then cover and reduce heat to low. Cook until rice is tender and liquid is absorbed, 15–20 minutes. Remove from heat, uncover, and let sit to allow excess moisture to evaporate, 5–10 minutes.
Fluff rice with a fork; pluck out and discard bay leaf. Transfer to a shallow bowl to serve.

aromatic-wilted-greens-with-coconut-milk.name: Aromatic Wilted Greens With Coconut Milk

instructions: Heat oil in a large skillet over medium. Cook garlic, stirring, until golden brown, about 3 minutes. Add shallots, chile, ginger, and two-thirds of scallions. Season with salt; cook, stirring often, until softened, about 5 minutes. Add kale and collard greens a handful at a time, letting them wilt slightly before adding more. Cook, tossing occasionally, until greens are crisp-tender, 6–8 minutes. Add half of coconut milk and toss to coat.
Transfer greens to a serving bowl and top with remaining coconut milk and scallions. Serve with lime wedges.

arouca-ball-teleculinaria.name: Arouca Ball

Lay out the dough crown in a round shape, previously greased with butter, and let it be let down until the volume is doubled.

Then dissolve some sugar into the water and brush the crown with this mixture. Sprinkle with powdered sugar and oven preheated at 180oC for about 30 to 40 minutes. Take it off, let it cool down a bit, unshape it and serve it.

Then mold the chestnuts with the help of flour and let it cool completely. Brush them with the crushed yolk and burn the surface with a kitchen torch or put them in the oven.

Pearl point Fill a spoon with the syrup and slowly pour it into a container. If, as it falls, it forms a somewhat resistant thread and, at its end, a ball appears, the syrup has reached the pearl point.

arrachera-mexican-skirt-steak-for-tacos.name: Arrachera (mexican Skirt Steak For Tacos)
instructions: Place a single layer of steak in a shallow casserole dish. Sprinkle a portion of lime juice, sazón, and adobo seasoning. Top with chopped onion pieces. Repeat layers until all pieces of steak are seasoned. Pour beer over the mixture. Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour, and up to 24 hours.
Preheat a grill to medium-high.
Discard marinade. Grill steak until lightly charred on the outside but still slightly tender in the center for medium doneness, 6 to 7 minutes per side.

arroz-chaufa-chinese-peruvian-rice-peque.name: Arroz Chaufa, Chinese Peruvian Rice

arroz-con-gandules.name: Arroz Con Gandules

ingredients.item: Sazón with culantro and achiote seasoning

instructions: To the bowl of a food processor, add the cilantro, culantro, onion, red and green bell peppers and garlic. Pulse until combined. Add the cumin and oregano and pulse a few more times to incorporate. The sofrito should have the consistency of a tapenade.

Heat ¼ cup of canola oil in a large pan over medium-high heat. When the oil is shimmering, add the sofrito and fry, stirring constantly, until deepened in color but not brown, about 3 minutes. Add the olives, tomato sauce, adobo seasoning, Sazón seasoning, pigeon peas, and water, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then add the rice. Cook for 15–20 minutes, until the liquid is fully evaporated and the rice around the edges of the pot looks dry.
Stir, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and cook the rice for another 30 minutes, stirring every 10 minutes, until the rice is tender and fluffy.
Serve the arroz con gandules warm alongside dishes of your choice.
Enjoy!

Bring ham hock and stock to a simmer in a medium saucepan over medium-low heat, cover, and cook until ham hock is tender, at least 1 hour or up to 2.

Add Sazón, salt, and pepper and cook, stirring, until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add rice and olives, pimientos, and capers and cook, stirring often, until some grains are translucent, about 3 minutes. Add ham hock and stock (you should have 3 cups; if not, add water or more stock to get you there). Scatter pigeon peas over. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to medium-low. Cover saucepan and simmer until rice is tender and liquid is absorbed, 20–30 minutes.
Remove pan from heat, transfer ham hock to a plate, and fluff rice with a fork. Cover pan and let sit 15 minutes. Shred meat from ham hock, cover loosely, and let sit until ready to serve.
Transfer rice to a platter. Top with reserved ham hock and cilantro.

Return the rice to the pot and stir in the whole milk, Bring to boil, then reduce the heat to
